articles.title: One Person Who Appears to Be Missing From King Charles’s U.S. Itinerary: Prince Harry
articles.summary: On a state visit designed in part to repair U.S.-British relations, King Charles’s schedule does not include plans to see his younger son, who lives in the United States with his family.

articles.title: Trump Administration Secures New Indictment Against James Comey
articles.summary: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.

articles.title: FCC Orders a Review of ABC’s Licenses Amid Feud Between Trump and Kimmel
articles.summary: The agency said the review was related to the network’s diversity and inclusion policies. But it came amid a fight between the president and the network’s late night host, Jimmy Kimmel.

articles.title: With no radical footprint, what drove suspect to try and assassinate Trump?
articles.summary: An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.

articles.title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.

articles.title: King Charles urges 'reconciliation' in address to U.S. Congress amid Trump tensions
articles.summary: In an address to the U.S. Congress, King Charles told lawmakers that "time and again, our two countries have always found ways to come together," and called for "reconciliation and renewal."

articles.title: What Is OPEC, and How Does the Oil Cartel Influence Petroleum Prices?
articles.summary: The group of oil-producing countries supplied more than 25 percent of the world’s oil before the war in Iran. Its members have influenced energy markets through the years.

articles.title: Justice Department indicts former FBI director James Comey for a second time
articles.summary: The case revolves around a photo the former FBI director posted online last year of seashells on a beach arranged to say "8647."

articles.title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
articles.summary: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.

articles.title: As trial against OpenAI begins, Elon Musk seeks Sam Altman's ouster
articles.summary: If Musk gets what he's asking for, it would radically re-shape one of the world's leading AI companies.

articles.title: Trump's 'American flag blue' reflecting pool project gets a mixed reaction in D.C.
articles.summary: The pool is being resurfaced in a shade more akin to that of a swimming pool. It's one of many physical changes Trump is planning for the nation's capital.

articles.title: UAE Says It Will Leave OPEC as Iran War Strains Oil Markets
articles.summary: The Gulf government has long complained about the group’s quotas, which officials believe unfairly limited its exports. Its departure is expected to weaken OPEC’s influence.

articles.title: Elon Musk Assails Sam Altman on Social Media Before OpenAI Trial
articles.summary: Mr. Musk’s posts on X have pushed his narrative that OpenAI has lost its way.

articles.title: South Korean court extends prison sentence for wife of ousted president
articles.summary: In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.

articles.title: Natural disasters can cause another crisis for those recovering from opioid addiction
articles.summary: People recovering from opioid addiction risk relapse when they can't get their medications after natural disasters. A group of doctors is calling for lawmakers to ease access to the meds.

articles.title: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC oil cartel after nearly 60 years
articles.summary: The Arab oil producer has long expressed frustration with the quotas it has to follow as part of OPEC, the cartel of major state-owned oil producers.

articles.title: WHCA dinner shooter charged. And, Charles III to address Congress
articles.summary: Cole Allen, the man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, is being charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. And, King Charles III is set to address Congress today.

articles.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
articles.summary: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of conte

articles.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
articles.summary: 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.

articles.title: Violence escalates in Colombia with dozens of attacks before presidential vote
articles.summary: A spate of attacks against civilians and military bases in Colombia's southwestern region has raised security concerns as the country heads to a May presidential election.

articles.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption
articles.summary: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
